name: Complete Insurance Backend
description:
Implement the complete Insurance Claim Processing System backend in phases. The user wants a "completed backend" that does NOT include CQRS/MediatR, keeping it simple with traditional N-Tier architecture.

Phases to implement:
1. Foundation: Seed data, Serilog, Swagger, JWT setup, Program.cs configuration
2. Auth: Full authentication system including JWT, refresh tokens, account lockout, role-based authorization, KYC
3. Policy: Full CRUD, approval/rejection, status transitions, coverage tracking
4. Claims: Submission, review, approval/rejection, payout calculation, reviewer auto-assignment, workflow history
5. Documents: Upload/download with validation, soft delete, WebP conversion
6. Notifications: Email (MailKit + MailHog), in-app notification inbox
7. Payments: Stripe PaymentIntent, payment processing, link to claim close
8. Premium payments: Grace period logic, policy lapse tracking
9. Tests: Unit tests for major services + one integration test for full claim lifecycle

What's already done:
- Domain layer (entities, enums, events, exceptions)
- Application layer (Common pattern classes, DTOs, repository interfaces, service interfaces)
- Infrastructure layer (DbContext, configurations, repositories, UnitOfWork, migration)
- API middleware (GlobalExceptionMiddleware, CorrelationIdMiddleware)

What's needed:
- Application service implementations (AuthService, PolicyService, ClaimService, etc.)
- FluentValidation validators
- AutoMapper profiles
- API controllers
- JWT authentication configuration in Program.cs
- Seed data (Admin, PolicyTypes, ClaimTypes, PolicyBenefitRules)
- External service implementations (Email, FileStorage, Stripe)
- Hangfire background jobs
- SignalR notification hub
- Tests

Requirements:
- Use implementation_plan.md as single source of truth
- No CQRS/MediatR - simple N-Tier with service interfaces injected into controllers
- PostgreSQL, EF Core code-first
- Clean, modular, testable code
- Thin controllers, business logic in services
- Production-quality code with proper error handling
- At least 70% test coverage for services

goal.answer: Complete the InsuranceClaimSystem backend: implement all Application services, controllers, auth middleware, external services (email, file, Stripe), validators, AutoMapper profiles, Hangfire jobs, SignalR hub, seed data, and unit/integration tests. Build atop all existing code without repeating completed work. Delivery excludes CQRS/MediatR and frontend code.

criteria.answer:
All service interfaces have concrete implementations with correct DI registration
All API controllers exist and expose every required endpoint from implementation_plan.md
JWT authentication is working with 15-min access + 7-day refresh tokens in httpOnly cookies
Role-based authorization works for all 5 roles with policy-based guards
Build compiles with 0 errors
Seed data creates Admin, PolicyTypes, ClaimTypes, and PolicyBenefitRules on startup
Claim state machine enforces valid transitions submitted→review→approve→paid→closed
Payout formula implemented: MIN(ClaimedAmount, SubLimit) × (1 - CoPayPercent/100) - Deductible
Auto-assign reviewer uses specialization match → lowest workload → round-robin logic
Stripe PaymentIntent creation confirmation and idempotency protection works
RemainingCoverageAmount decrements on approved claim payment
Grace period: 15 days for Monthly, 30 days for others
MailKit SMTP email sends for all 18 notification events
Unit test coverage for AuthService ClaimService PolicyService ≥70%
Integration test covers full claim lifecycle Submit → Review → Approve → Pay → Closed

constraints.answer: No CQRS/MediatR - use simple N-Tier service layer, No SystemHealthLogs (out of scope), No frontend code, Use only packages already referenced in csproj files, Keep controllers thin - max 30 lines per action, Must build on top of existing code - don't redo what's done

phases.answer: Foundation, Auth, Policy, Claims, Payments & Notifications, Tests

assumptions.answer: PostgreSQL is installed locally and accessible; dotnet-ef tool is available; MailHog is available for local email testing; Stripe test keys can be configured in appsettings; No Angular frontend is needed for this backend scope; All existing code (entities, repositories, DTOs, middleware) is accepted as-is

phases.name: Foundation
phases.goal: Wire up the cross-cutting infrastructure and external services

steps.description: Wire Program.cs with all required services: CORS, JWT auth, rate limiting, exception middleware, Serilog, Swagger, Hangfire, SignalR, AutoMapper, FluentValidation
Create AutoMapper profiles for entity↔DTO mapping
Create all FluentValidation validators for request DTOs
Add seed data for Admin user, PolicyTypes, ClaimTypes, PolicyBenefitRules

Create external service interfaces in Application layer and implementations in Infrastructure layer: IEmailService/SmtpEmailService, IFileStorageService/LocalFileStorageService, IStripeService/StripePaymentService, IPiiEncryptionService/PiiEncryptionService
Implement JwtTokenService for access/refresh token generation and validation
Implement AuthService (register, login, refresh, forgot/reset password, email verification), AccountService (profile, password), AddressService (CRUD)
Create AuthController, AccountController, AdminController with all endpoints
Configure JWT auth in Program.cs with custom policies for all 5 roles and account lockout rules

phases.name: Policy
phases.goal: Implement policy lifecycle management and premium tracking

steps.description: Implement PolicyService (create, get, update, delete, approve, reject) and PremiumPaymentService
Create PolicyController with full CRUD endpoints
Implement Hangfire background jobs for premium reminders, grace period lapse, and policy expiry

phases.name: Claims
phases.goal: Implement claims lifecycle and document management

steps.description: Implement ClaimValidationService (all 9 validations) and ClaimService (submit, get, update status, auto-assign reviewer, approve, reject, payout calc)
Implement DocumentService (upload, download, verify, soft delete) and ClaimStateMachine
Create ClaimsController, DocumentsController, ReviewersController with all endpoints

phases.name: Payments & Notifications
phases.goal: Implement payment processing and notification system

steps.description: Implement PaymentService with Stripe PaymentIntent creation, confirmation, and idempotency protection
Create PaymentsController with premium and claim payment endpoints
Implement NotificationService with SMTP email, SignalR hub, in-app notifications
Create NotificationHub and NotificationsController for inbox and mark-as-read

phases.name: Tests
phases.goal: Write unit and integration tests for core services

steps.description: Create test project and write unit tests for AuthService
Write unit tests for ClaimService
Write unit tests for PolicyService
Write integration test for full claim lifecycle Submit→Review→Approve→Pay→Closed
